  "findings": [
    "Mechanical excavation equipment was used within the designated utility tolerance zone instead of hand digging or vacuum excavation."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately halt all mechanical excavation within the tolerance zone.",
    "Instruct the crew to use soft excavation methods (hand digging or potholing/vacuum excavation) to expose the utility safely.",
    "Verify the integrity of any exposed utility lines before proceeding with work."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to 811 and Common Ground Alliance (CGA) Best Practices, mechanical excavation is strictly prohibited within the specified tolerance zone of marked utilities. Excavators must use non-destructive, soft digging methods (such as hand tools or vacuum excavation) to expose the utility line before any power-operated equipment can be used in that zone to prevent catastrophic utility strikes.",
  "references": [
    "CGA Best Practices Version 18.0, Chapter 5 (Excavation)",
    "OSHA 29 CFR 1926.651(b)"
  ]
}
